content:
A model predicts. A solution creates business value. The candidate is expected to know this and be able to explain.

10 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

A few years ago, if you were preparing for a data science or machine learning interview, you could expect questions like:

• “What is overfitting?”

• “How does random forest work?”

• “What is precision vs recall?”

• “How will you improve model accuracy?”
Don’t get me wrong. These questions are still super important. But modern day interviews have changed with AI being a critical element now.
Today, if you are interviewing for roles like ML Engineer, AI Solution Architect, AI Solutions Specialist, GenAI Consultant, AI Product Specialist, or Enterprise AI Lead, interviewers are not only checking whether you understand models.
They are also checking whether you understand solutions.
And this is where many candidates struggle.
They know how to explain machine learning models.
They know what LLMs are.
